incidents.vector: German Federal Criminal Police (BKA) identified and doxed Daniil Shchukin as leader of GandCrab and REvil ransomware groups responsible for 130+ attacks
incidents.impact: Leader of historically major ransomware groups publicly named; €35 million in economic damage attributed across Germany; suspect believed residing in Krasnodar, Russia.

incidents.vector: Data-wiping attack by Iran-backed hacktivist group erased data from over 200,000 systems, servers, and mobile devices across the company
incidents.impact: Offices in 79 countries shut down; over 5,000 workers sent home from Ireland hub; company data claimed stolen and threatened with release. Attack claimed as retaliation for a deadly missile strike on an Iranian school.

incidents.vector: Self-propagating CanisterWorm spread through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers; deploys wiper if Farsi language or Iran timezone detected
incidents.impact: Data destroyed on infected systems matching Iran's timezone or language settings; Kubernetes clusters wiped entirely if accessible. Wiper deployed by TeamPCP group using same infrastructure as their supply chain campaign.

incidents.vector: Exploited vulnerability (CVE-2026-3502) in TrueConf video conferencing updater to distribute malicious updates via compromised on-premises server serving dozens of government agencies
incidents.impact: Dozens of government entities received weaponized updates deploying Havoc and ShadowPad malware. CISA ordered federal agencies to patch within two weeks.

incidents.vector: Russian hackers revisiting previously compromised infrastructure to check for unpatched vulnerabilities and still-valid credentials; using social engineering via phone calls with Ukrainian numbers and fluent Ukrainian
incidents.impact: Successful re-access in some cases where root causes were not fully remediated. Shift from quick data theft to maintaining long-term network presence targeting Ukraine's security and defense sector.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of React2Shell vulnerability (CVE-2025-55182) in Next.js to deploy NEXUS Listener multi-phase credential harvesting framework with web GUI for operators
incidents.impact: 766+ hosts confirmed compromised across multiple cloud providers. Mass exfiltration of credentials, SSH keys, and cloud tokens enabling downstream lateral movement into cloud environments.

incidents.vector: BRICKSTORM malware exploiting weak vSphere identity configurations to establish persistence at the virtualization layer below guest OS, entirely outside EDR visibility
incidents.impact: Full administrative control of vSphere environments; ability to access raw VM disk storage bypassing OS-level security; attackers can power off VMs and reset ESXi root credentials. No standard EDR coverage at this layer.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on town and public safety computer systems serving multiple northern Massachusetts towns
incidents.impact: Non-emergency and business phone lines knocked offline; 9-1-1 still functional. Towns of Pepperell, Ashby, Dunstable, and Groton affected. Federal law enforcement notified.

incidents.vector: Hijacked npm account of lead Axios maintainer to publish malicious versions (v1.14.1, v0.30.4) deploying WAVESHAPER.V2 backdoor via hidden dependency across Windows, macOS, and Linux
incidents.impact: Malicious versions live on npm for ~3 hours affecting library with 100M+ weekly downloads. Estimated 600,000 compromised downloads. Google attributed to North Korea-linked UNC1069.

incidents.vector: Targeted attacks against messaging apps (WhatsApp, Signal, Messenger) using social engineering, phishing links, and QR codes to add attacker devices to victim accounts
incidents.impact: UK NCSC and international partners issued joint warning. Attackers trick victims into sharing login codes or silently joining group chats. Government officials specifically targeted.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of critical out-of-bounds read vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) in Citrix NetScaler ADC enabling unauthenticated memory disclosure, similar to prior CitrixBleed attacks
incidents.impact: Sensitive memory leaked from NetScaler deployments. CISA added to KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch. Third major Citrix memory disclosure vulnerability in three years.

incidents.vector: Criminal group claimed breach and released 3 GB data archive for free after failing to find buyers via encrypted messaging
incidents.impact: Partial verification by researchers shows internal developer data, employee records across clinical research subsidiaries, and source code structures. AstraZeneca has not confirmed or denied at 96+ hours. Potential GDPR and HIPAA exposure.

incidents.vector: Unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2025-53521) in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager actively exploited in the wild
incidents.impact: Active exploitation confirmed by F5 and NCSC. All organizations using BIG-IP APM affected. CISA and NCSC issued urgent alerts; organizations advised to investigate for compromise regardless of patch status.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to network; specific vector undisclosed in SEC 8-K material breach filing
incidents.impact: Systems taken offline; SEC Item 1.05 material breach disclosure filed. Order processing operational but may face delays. Full remediation expected to take several weeks.

incidents.vector: Breach of the European Commission's Amazon Web Services account on March 24, detected and blocked
incidents.impact: Internal data potentially stolen. Internal investigation ongoing to establish extent of the breach. Second cyber incident affecting the Commission in recent months.

incidents.vector: Compromise of FBI Director's personal email account by Iran-backed hacktivist group; data published for public download in retaliation for U.S. actions against the group
incidents.impact: Personal emails, conversations, and documents exposed. FBI confirmed targeting of personal email but said information is historical with no government data. State Department offering $10M reward for group members.

incidents.victim: Hundreds of organizations across multiple sectors
incidents.vector: AI-generated phishing lures distributed via Railway PaaS infrastructure exploiting Microsoft device authentication flow to steal OAuth tokens valid for 90 days without passwords or MFA
incidents.impact: Over 344 confirmed victims including construction, law firms, healthcare, and government. Unique AI-crafted lures at scale with no identical emails or domains. Over 50 new compromises daily at peak.

incidents.vector: Russian initial access broker sold unauthorized network access to ransomware groups including Yanluowang, facilitating dozens of attacks across the United States
incidents.impact: Aleksei Volkov sentenced to 81 months in prison. $9 million actual losses, $24 million intended losses across victims. Extradited from Italy; ordered to pay $9.2 million restitution.

incidents.victim: Software supply chain (Trivy, Checkmarx, LiteLLM, Telnyx, and 48+ packages)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP group compromised GitHub Actions and package registries to inject credential-stealing malware into trusted open-source security tools; launched CanisterWorm for propagation and dual ransomware operations
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 SaaS environments affected; 300+ GB of credentials, cloud tokens, and SSH keys stolen; 16 organizations publicly extorted. Databricks investigating alleged downstream compromise. CISA KEV deadline April 8.

incidents.vector: Password spraying attacks against Microsoft 365 accounts in three distinct waves correlated with cities targeted by Iranian missile strikes
incidents.impact: Active compromise attempts against governments managing missile strike response. Assessed as bombing damage assessment intelligence gathering to support kinetic military operations.

incidents.victim: City of Minot water treatment plant
incidents.vector: Ransomware deployed on water treatment plant systems; no direct ransom demand or interaction beyond a message displayed on screen
incidents.impact: Manual water treatment procedures for 16 hours requiring on-location gauge checks. Water remained safe throughout. Affected server unplugged; FBI investigating.
